Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

OTHER/MOVANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To Intervene.

Proposed intervenor State Compensation Insurance Fund's motion for order granting leave to intervene is granted. No opposition filed.

Counsel for State Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and is verbatim with the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Continued To Oct-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Cross-Complainant Paul Ryan Associates Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

CROSS DEFENDANT INTERIOR WOOD SYSTEMS, INC.'s Joinder To Cross-Complainant Paul Ryan Associates Motion To Compel is OFF CALENDAR in light of the ruling on the motion sought to be joined. [302]

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Medical Examinations
Off Calendar Apr-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Richard Jacobson, Esq. of Jacobson Markham LLP, T:916.854.5969, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Jessica Dewitt, Esq. of Brady Law Group, T:800.881.9011, for plaintiff/opposing party.

Prior to the hearing, Judge Kahn disclosed that at a recent function of the SF Trial Lawyers' Association, he sat at the table of the Brady Law Group and met Ms. DeWitt. He does not believe that this will affect his ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No questions were asked or objections raised.

RULING: Hearing held. After hearing the Court ruled on DEFENDANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES' Motion For Medical Examinations, as follows:

Mr. Hernandez may be examined for a maximum of 3.5 hours exclusive of breaks by Dr. Strassberg and for a maximum of 6 hours exclusive of breaks by Dr. Hooker. Mr. Hernandez can take as many breaks as he wants. Any duplicative questions shall be stricken; raw data need to be provided to plaintiff's expert; plaintiff may audio record the proceedings.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Medical Examinations
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Continued To Apr-09-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard Jacobson, Esq. of JACOBSON MARKHAM LLP, Tel. No. 916.854.5969, appearing for the Defendant Paul Ryan Associates/Moving Party;
Elizabeth D. Rhodes, Esq. of CLAPP MORONEY VUCINICH BEEMAN SCHELEY, Tel. No. 650.989.5400, appearing for Cross-Defendant Interior Wood System, Inc./Joining Party;
Jessica DeWitt, Esq. of BRADY LAW GROUP, Tel No. 415.429.7300, appearing for the Plaintiff Luis Hernnadez.

Matter called, discussions regarding the language in the proposed order are held. The matter is passed for the proposed order to be reviewed by Ms. DeWitt.

At the second call of the matter, Plaintiff's Counsel DOES NOT stipulate to this motion being heard by Judge Pro Tem Stephanie Krmpotic. Argument is held.

There being a non-stip to the Judge Pro Tem, at the conclusion of argument Defendant Paul Ryan Associates' Motion For Medical Examinations is continued to April 9, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. A report and recommendation will be prepared by Judge Pro Tem Stephanie Krmpotic.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT)
